titlestmt.titleproper: Wollaston, Quincy, Mass. Wollaston Congregational Church records, 1874-2025.
titlestmt.author: Madison Bellew

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday March 19, 2026 at 16:49

did.unittitle: Wollaston, Quincy, Mass. Wollaston Congregational Church records, 1874-2025.
did.abstract: The Wollaston Congregational Church in Wollaston, Quincy, MA was formed from the Wollaston Congregation Society in 1876 with its church building being dedicated that same year. As the church grew, it purchased a parsonage in 1891, built a parish house in 1915, and replaced the original church building with a new one in 1926. The church was renovated in the 1960s, 2009, and 2018 to improve its facilities and ensure the historic preservation of the church building. The church reached its high point in the 1950s and 1960s before continuously declining in both membership and finances even though there were efforts to improve throughout the years, specifically in 2007 with Rev. Gifford's turnaround ministry. However, the church continued to decline and voted to become a legacy church in 2024 with the building sold to the city of Quincy and the last service taking place December 15, 2024. The collection contains records relating to the administration, history, members, and activities of Wollaston Congregational Church including programs, scrapbooks, financial records, minutes, reports, audiovisual material, photos, legal records, building records, correspondence, vital statistics, and other documents.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to this collection is unrestricted and open to the public. The library reserves the right to restrict access to identifying information.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use
userestrict.p: Items in this collection are subject to U.S. Copyright Law. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine the copyright status of collection items and to secure any permissions necessary for their reproduction and publication. Requests for permission to publish material must be discussed with the archivist or librarian.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], in the Wollaston, Quincy, Mass. Wollaston Congregational Church records, 1874-2025, RG1048. The Congregational Library & Archives, Boston, MA.

acqinfo.head: Acquisition Information
acqinfo.p: Materials were donated to the Congregational Library & Archives by Wollaston Congregational Church in May 2025; 2025-12.

Twelve volumes were donated by Wollaston Congregational Church in February 1962. Seven additional volumes from the women's groups were donated in August 1968. Some materials including various programs and a history of Wollaston Congregational Church were donated in 1926, 1952, 1957, and 1975.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: An archivist, Julia Collins, working at Wollaston Congregational Church processed portions of the collection in 2004-2005.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was reprocessed by Madison Bellew, March 2026, according to the rules of Describing Archives: A Content Standard. Portions of the collection were originally processed by G McFarland, March 2023 using DACS Second Edition.

bioghist.head: Historical Note
bioghist.p: The Wollaston Congregational Church in Wollaston, Quincy, MA was formed from the Wollaston Congregational Society which was organized in November 1874. In 1875, the Ladies Benevolent Society was formed, and construction began on a church building with the cornerstone laid in October 1875. In 1876, the Sunday school started at the beginning of the year. Also in 1876, the church was officially recognized by an ecclesiastical council in May with the building being dedicated at the same time and Rev. Francis Zabriskee being the first pastor. As the church grew, they were able to build a parsonage on Marion St in 1891 and a parish house in 1915. In 1925, the church voted to build a new building with the old building being destroyed, and the cornerstone of the new building was laid June 1925. Construction finished on the new church building in 1926 with a dedication in March and the stained-glass memorial window dedicated separately in October 1926. In 1951, the church celebrated 75 years and burned its mortgage as part of the festivities. In the 1960s, the church joined the United Church of Christ in 1960 and organized and completed a renovation of the Sunday school and gym facilities between 1962 and 1966. After the 1950s and 1960s, the church's membership and finances began to decline and could not regain the highest numbers seen previously.

Entering the 21st century, membership and finances continued to spiral downward. In 1998 and 1999, the church sold the land surrounding it. In 2007, the church decided to become a turnaround church under Rev. Mary Louise Gifford in hopes of combating the decline so that the church could remain open and prosperous. This change came with a change in church governance to a board of stewards which replaced the previous council that had replaced the administrative committees a few years earlier. In 2008, the church building was listed on the National Register of Historic Places, and in both 2009 and 2018, the church underwent renovations to ensure the historic preservation of the building. As the 2000s continued, the membership and finances declined further until the church voted to become a legacy church in January 2024. The church sold the building to the city of Quincy at the end of 2024 to be used as a community center. On December 15, 2024 the church held its last service and officially closed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains records relating to the administration, activities, members, and history of Wollaston Congregational Church including annual reports, financial records, legal records, building records, correspondence, minutes, by-laws, newsletters, programs and calendars, vital statistics, publicity, histories, yearbooks, directories, and anniversary and event records. The collection includes volumes, pamphlets, account books, checkbooks, registers, guest books, song books, plans, photographs, photo slides, VHS tapes, CDs, flash drives, cassettes, vinyl records, scrapbooks, newspapers, and other documents.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged into six series with three of those series being furthered arranged into subseries. The series and subseries are arranged chronologically by start date then alphabetically.

phystech.head: Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Various media players and hardware are required to view the audiovisual material in the collection.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Some lantern slides, clippings, and other records can be found as part of the Warren S. Parker collection at the Thomas Crane Public Library with digital scans of the images within the Parker collection available at Digital Commonwealth. Additional artifacts and records may be available at the Quincy Historical Society.

odd.head: Content Warning
odd.p: This collection includes content and language that may be upsetting or triggering. These records reflect the language and attitudes of the historical period in which they were created; many of these terms are unacceptable today. The Congregational Library & Archives works to preserve Congregational history and stories while acknowledging that sometimes that history is problematic or questionable. We do this to preserve, contextualize, and reflect on the past so that we may learn from it.
This collection contains racist images and language.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains records relating to the administration of Wollaston Congregational Church including minutes, combined minutes and register volumes, financial records, annual reports, building and renovation documents, legal documents, correspondence, by-laws, plans, and other related documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains annual reports for the church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains financial records including account books, financial reports, ledgers, donations, estate documents, bank documents and correspondence, grant applications and other related documents.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Materials removed from this volume for preservation purposes are in the folder "Fire insurance and balances, 1935-1939". The accounting note in the folder was discovered on page 179 of the volume.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: This file has materials removed for preservation purposes from the volume, "Account book, 1927-1943". The accounting note in the folder was discovered on page 179 of the volume.

did.unittitle: General, benevolence, and other funds account ledger
General and other funds account ledger
Funding for leadership for new and renewing congregations application
Lilly foundation transition into ministry grant
Church and society minutes and members

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material was removed from this volume and placed in file, "Society records and correspondence, 1890-1950" for preservation purposes.

did.unittitle: Deeds, estates, and legal records given to bank

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material in this file was removed from the volume, "Society minutes and members, 1874-1925" for preservation purposes.

did.unittitle: Wollaston map, portrait, and sunday school improvement
Young people and church school list, correspondence, and programs
Vacation school reports, correspondence, and handouts
Inventory and correspondence of records at bank
Remodeling fund program, correspondence, and publicity
Sunday school and gym alterations specifications
Sunday school and gym renovation specifications
Restoration final report with project manual and construction plans
Preservation projects application and restoration report

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Materials in this file were removed from the volume, "Stewards minutes, 2009-2012" for preservation purposes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains records related to or created by the various auxiliary groups at Wollaston Congregational Church including the prudential committee which became the trustees, the executive committee, the building committee, the board of stewards which replaced the various administrative committees as the main form of church governance in the early 2000s, some social clubs, and various other committees. The records include minutes, correspondence, reports, financial statements, ledgers, and other related records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains records from the social groups at Wollaston Congregational Church with the majority of the records relating to the mr. and mrs. club which was renamed the circle of friends in 1980. The records include minutes, correspondence, pamphlet yearbooks, programs, constitutions, histories, and financial ledgers.

did.unittitle: Mr. and mrs. club treasurer's book
Mr. and mrs. club pops concert
Mr. and mrs. club histories and anniversary invitations
Mr. and mrs. club treasurer's book
Executive and pastoral supply committee minutes

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Materials removed from this volume are stored separately in file titled "Debt of Honor Campaign program, 1938". The materials were found with the volume entry for February 27, 1938.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: This file was removed from the volume, "Exeuctive and pastoral supply committee minutes, 1932-1939". The material was found with the volume entry for February 27, 1938.

did.unittitle: Sunday school facilities study committee reports
Building committee correspondence, reports, and records
Building committee correspondence, reports, and minutes

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material in this file was removed from the volume, "Building committee minutes, 1963-1970" for preservation purposes.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material was removed from this volume for preservation purposes and placed in the file, "Building committee correspondence, reports, and minutes, 1963-1970".

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material was removed from this volume and placed in the file, "Executive committee minutes, 1969-1974" for preservation purposes.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material in this file was removed from the volume, "Executive committee minutes, 1967-1974" for preservation purposes.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material from this volume was removed and placed in the file, "Trustees' minutes, 1972-1974" for preservation purposes.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material in this file was removed from the volume, "Trustees' minutes, 1969-1974" for preservation purposes.

did.unittitle: Trustees' and inter-church council minutes and reports

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material in this file was seperated from the material in the file, "Executive committee minutes and directory, 1992-1994" for preservation purposes.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material in this file was seperated from material in the file, "Trustees' and inter-church council minutes and reports, 1990-1994" for preservation purposes.

did.unittitle: Executive committee, christian education, and deacons minutes
Trustees' and inter-church council minutes and reports
Stewards minutes, financial statements, and annual reports
Stewards minutes, financial statements, and annual reports
Stewards minutes, financial statements, and annual reports
Stewards minutes, financial statements, and annual reports
Stewards minutes, financial statements, and annual reports
Stewards minutes, financial statements, and annual meeting
Stewards minutes, financial statements, and annual reports
Stewards minutes, financial statements, and annual reports
Stewards minutes, financial statements, and annual reports
Stewards minutes, financial statements, and annual reports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains material relating to the history of the church and material published by or for the church that relates to its activites, events, and history. This series includes newsletters, programs, scrapbooks, photos, song books, newspapers, histories, historian records, anniversaries, brochures, and other documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains the church's newsletter which was orginally named "The Parish News" before being renamed as "The Wollaston Spire" or "The Spire" for short.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains loose service and concert programs, and bound volumes of service programs with each volume representing one year of services at the church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains scrapbooks with material dating from the founding of the church until the early 2000s including programs, photos, reports, newspapers, and other ephemera from the church.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material in this file was removed from the volume, "Scrapbook, 1948-1951" for preservation purposes.

did.unittitle: Copy 2 of "review of its first 75 years"
Copy 1 of "review of its first 75 years"
Stained glass memorial window history and sermons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains vital statistics and records relating to the members of the church including guest books, marriage forms and certificates, funeral records, directories, registers, and member cards. Some additional registers and member records are in series 1: administrative records as they are combined with various minutes and documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains records from the various women's groups at the church including minutes, reports, annual reports, correspondence, histories, account books, check books, by-laws, yearbook pamhlets, financial records, event records, and other documents. The women's groups began with the Ladies Benevolent Society in 1875 before it joined with the Missionary Society to form the Woman's Union in 1918, later renamed the Women's Union which had various groups within it including the loyalty, service, and business womens groups among others.

did.unittitle: Ladies benevolent society minutes and members

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Materials removed from this volume were placed in the folder, "Women's union history and correspondence, 1901-1968" for preservation purposes. The letter from the Calhoun Colored School was discovered on page 92 of this volume.

did.unittitle: Ladies benevolent society minutes, members, and reports

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material from this volume was removed and placed in the folder, "Correspondence and home missionary association forms, 1911-1913" for preservation purposes.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: This file has material removed from the volume, "Ladies benevolent society minutes and members, 1875-1892". The letter from the Calhoun Colored School was discovered on page 92 of the volume.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and home missionary association forms

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Materials in this file were removed from the volume, "Ladies benevolent society minutes, members, and reports, 1900-1916" for preservation purposes.

did.unittitle: Ladies benevolent society minutes and reports

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material from this volume was removed and placed in the folder, "Ladies benevolent society correspondence and annual reports, 1917-1918".

did.unittitle: Ladies benevolent society correspondence and annual reports

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Materials in this file were removed from the volume, "Ladies benevolent society minutes and reports, 1916-1918" for preservation purposes.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material from this volume was removed and placed in the folder, "Norfolk and pilgrim branch wbm report, 1919". The material was disovered on page 13 of this volume.

did.unittitle: Norfolk and pilgrim branch wbm report

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: This file is comprised of a brochure removed from the volume, "Woman's Union meeting minutes, 1918-1921". The item was disovered on page 13 of the volume.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Materials removed from this volume are stored separately in the folder "Reports, events, and correspondence, 1922-1923". Clipping titled "Our Slogan" and Ladies' Night ticket was discovered on page 184 of the volume. Letter from Dorothy H. Taylor on Boston University Women Graduates' Club letterhead was discovered on page 238 of the volume.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: This file is comprised of materials removed from the volume, "Woman's union minutes, 1921-1926". Clipping titled "Our Slogan" and Ladies' Night ticket was discovered on page 184 of the volume. Letter from Dorothy H. Taylor on Boston University Women Graduates' Club letterhead was discovered on page 238 of the volume.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Materials removed from this volume are stored separately in the folder, "woman's union records, 1932". Receipts were discovered on page 129 of this volume. The letter from Chaplain William S. Kelley was discovered on page 211 of this volume.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: This file has materials removed from the volume, "Woman's union minutes, 1926-1935". Receipts were discovered on page 129 of the volume. The letter from Chaplain William S. Kelley was discovered on page 211 of the volume.

did.unittitle: Women's union receipts and disbursements book
Women's union check book and treasurer's statements
Women's union receipts and disbursements book

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material was removed from this volume and placed in the folder, "Women's union financial reports and regulations, 1950-1960" for preservation purposes.

did.unittitle: Women's union luncheon supplies and expenses
Women's union financial reports and regulations

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Material in this file was removed from the volume, "Women's union receipts and disbursements book, 1944-1959" for preservation purposes.

did.unittitle: Women's union by-laws and standing rules
Loyalty group minutes, members, and activities
Women's union officers and financial reports
Evening guild financial records, members, and programs
Loyalty group account book and obituaries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains various audiovisual material including photos, photo slides, photo negatives, VHS tapes, cassettee tapes, vinyl records, CDs, and flashdrives. The CDs and flashdrives are mostly photos, but also include videos, text files, and powerpoints. The digital files include minutes, reports, events, trips, and other records.

did.unittitle: "Dear old new england" by wollaston singers
"Joy to the world" by quincy bell ringers
New church building and cornerstone laying
Miss MacCauley's sunday school photo slides
Installation of officers and paraments dedication photos
Celebration of life of the rev. mr. Lloyd F. Martin
50th anniversary of the ordination of rev. Elden Zuern
Cleveland trip, wedding, vacation school, and member photos
